expectations.text: layout.txt organizes by feature (auth/, billing/, users/) rather than primarily by horizontal layer
expectations.evidence: src/features/ is labeled 'Vertical slices' and contains auth/, users/, billing/ each owning routes/service/repo/schemas/errors.

expectations.text: layout.txt colocates test files next to source files rather than in a distant tests/ tree
expectations.evidence: Tests live in a separate top-level tests/ tree that mirrors src/ one-to-one (tests/features/auth/auth.service.test.ts), not colocated with source files.

expectations.text: layout.txt contains concrete domain directories like auth, billing, or users
expectations.evidence: Layout includes features/auth/, features/users/, and features/billing/ directories explicitly.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explicitly addresses naming — avoiding generic names or requiring domain-specific names
expectations.evidence: Section 2 covers casing, suffixes, and DTO/Input/Query conventions but does not forbid generic names (utils, helpers, handler) or mandate domain-specific verbs.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md gives a concrete file-size guideline with a number
expectations.evidence: Section 3: 'SKILL ceiling: 250 lines per file' plus 'routes files ... max ~150 lines' and 'Functions: max 40 lines.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md requires type annotations on public / module-boundary functions
expectations.evidence: Section 4: 'Return types required on exported functions. Inferred internally is fine.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md mentions tests/lint/typecheck as the verification loop
expectations.evidence: Section 12: 'PRs must pass: bun run typecheck, bun run lint, bun test, bun run build.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md discourages dynamic/metaprogramming patterns for agent-traceability
expectations.evidence: No ban on Proxy, Reflect, decorators, DI containers, or runtime metaprogramming; only enums and unchecked casts are restricted.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explains at least one decision in terms of AI-agent behavior
expectations.evidence: Header and Section 13 frame conventions around agents, including 'Agents read whole files; large files waste context.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md recommends branded / nominal types for IDs (e.g. UserId as branded string) — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 6 mentions ULID/UUID generation in lib/id.ts but never introduces branded or nominal ID types.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md prefers discriminated unions (or equivalent) over magic-string dispatch / string-keyed registries — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Discriminated unions are not mentioned and no guidance warns against magic-string dispatch or string-keyed registries.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md addresses dependency pinning, version pinning, or 'boring stack' / training-data hygiene — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 8: 'Pin exact versions in package.json (hono: 4.6.3, not ^4.6.3).'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md covers deterministic tests (seeded randomness, frozen time) OR injectable side effects (clock, fetch, env as arguments) — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 9 discusses factories and transaction isolation but never specifies seeded randomness, frozen clocks, or injectable clock/fetch/env dependencies.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explicitly discourages barrel / re-export index files — v1.3 guidance (AR011)
expectations.evidence: Section 13: 'Don't add index.ts barrels except in db/schema/.'
